task_completion.description: Verify that the survey point data from colorado_foothills.csv is loaded into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing imported point data in the workspace, or an import/open dialog box with 'colorado_foothills.csv' selected.

task_completion.description: Verify that a triangulated terrain model (TIN/MDT) has been generated from the points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal workspace displaying a visible mesh of triangles (TIN/MDT) connecting the imported survey points.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required metrics (3D area, 2D area, surface ratio, and number of triangles) are computed in TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A calculation result window, properties panel, or internal report dialog within TopoCal displaying values for the 3D surface area, 2D planimetric area, surface ratio, and total number of triangles.

task_completion.description: Verify that the TopoCal project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal window title bar displaying 'surface_area_project.top' or a 'Save As' dialog showing the file being saved to 'C:\workspace\data\surface_area_project.top'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the plain-text report is created with the required format and saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) displaying 'surface_area_report.txt' (or a file explorer showing the file at C:\workspace\data\) containing the exact keys: '2D_AREA_M2:', '3D_AREA_M2:', 'SURFACE_RATIO:', and 'NUM_TRIANGLES:' followed by their respective computed values.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the TopoCal application windows, menus, and dialogs, rather than just running a script in a terminal to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Verify the values in the final text report match the calculations performed in TopoCal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values entered into the 'surface_area_report.txt' file should match the values displayed in the TopoCal calculation/properties panels seen in earlier screenshots.

integrity.description: Verify the text report was actually saved to the file system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor window should show the correct file path/name in its title bar, or a file explorer window should show 'surface_area_report.txt' existing in the 'C:\workspace\data\' directory, not just an unsaved 'Untitled' document.
